serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Golden Stir-fried Corn is one small plate, weighing approximately 250 grams.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central for 'Corn, sweet, yellow, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt' and 'Butter, salted' to estimate a stir-fried corn dish with butter. Original input values were used as a guide where reasonable.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index for sweet corn is approximately 60 (medium GI). Glycemic load calculated based on 14g carbohydrate per 100g and a 250g serving size.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content, saturated fat, and potential for high-sodium additions
hypertension.guidance: Limit portion size and request omission of high-sodium ingredients like fish sauce or dried shrimp to manage blood pressure, and reduce butter to lower saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index of corn, added sugar, and saturated fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: Consume in moderation due to the moderate glycemic index of corn and added sugar; pairing with protein can help, and reducing butter intake is advisable to improve insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat content from butter and potential for high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Reduce butter content or substitute with healthier oils to lower saturated fat intake, and minimize high-sodium additions to support cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Golden Stir-fried Corn, known in Vietnamese as Bắp Xào Hoàng Kim, is a popular Vietnamese street food and side dish. It typically features sweet corn kernels stir-fried with butter, spring onions, and sometimes dried shrimp or other seasonings, which contribute to its rich flavor and 'golden' appearance. The dish has a sweet and savory profile with a slightly chewy texture from the corn. Nutritionally, it is moderate in calories and carbohydrates, but can be high in saturated fat due to butter and sodium if dried shrimp or fish sauce are included. It provides some dietary fiber and vitamin C from the corn.
common_user_queries: Is Bắp Xào Hoàng Kim healthy?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are an adaptation from USDA FoodData Central for individual ingredients (corn, butter) as an exact match for 'Golden Stir-fried Corn' was not available. GI is an analogous estimate.
notes: Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, vitamin A, B vitamins, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water values were estimated based on typical sweet corn and butter compositions from USDA FoodData Central to provide a more complete nutritional profile. The original input provided only a limited set of nutrients. Cholesterol and sodium values were retained from the input as they were plausible.
